Source.title: core/vm: fix overflow in gas calculation formula
Source.body:
This PR fixes an overflow occurring in the squaring-operation when calculating the square-coefficient of memory expansion. Values > `0xffffffffe1` would make the `square` = `0`. Fortunately, the linear coefficient still resulted in 12.8 billion gas, so it's not a critical issue. 

The PR now instead throws a gas overflow error for memory requirements larger than `0xffffffffe0` which is about 1.1 TB.
_commit_messages: core/vm: fix overflow in gas calculation formula
core/vm : fix testcase for gas calculation

Source.title: accounts/abi: include access-list in gas estimation
Source.body:
Simple bugfix to include the access-list in the gas-estimation step of the ABI bindings code.

With OP-Stack we stumbled on it during testing of a contract that reverts when access-list data is missing.
We fixed it in op-geth here: https://github.com/ethereum-optimism/op-geth/pull/546
_commit_messages: accounts/abi: include access-list in gas estimation

Source.title: eth, internal/ethapi: make RPC block miner field show block sealer correctly
Source.body:
If the poa consensus mechanism is used, the miner of each block will be 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000, modify the author method of returning to the engine.

RPC API:The miner returned by Block is consensus.Engine.Author(block.Header())

Also, implemented tests for:   
eth_getBlockByNumber eth_getBlockByHash

Source.title: trie: make fullnode children hash calculation concurrently
Source.body:
Motivation:

For fullnode in trie, the children hash calculation was serial before. However, the hash calculation between any child nodes does not affect each other. So i make this concurrently.

Performance:

I run the `BenchmarkHashBE` and `BenchmarkHashLE` on my server machine(8cores 3.7GHZ) for performance checking.

for parallel：

```
BenchmarkHash-8   	 1000000	      1868 ns/op	    1513 B/op	      18 allocs/op
```

for serial:

```
BenchmarkHash-8   	  500000	      3919 ns/op	    1428 B/op	      15 allocs/op
```

Performance doubled when hash large tree.
_commit_messages: trie: make fullnode children hash calculation concurrently
trie: thread out only on topmost fullnode
trie: clean up full node children hash calculation
trie: minor code fixups

Source.title: cmd/evm: change error msg output to stderr
Source.body:
use the `ctx.Writer` and `ctx.ErrWriter` as output witer,

before:

```
$> ./evm compile test.sol 2>/dev/null
test.sol:0 syntax error: unexpected hello, expected end of line
test.sol:0 syntax error: unexpected EOF, expected new line
```

after:

```
$> ./evm compile test.sol 2>/dev/null
```

Infestor.title: feat (internal/ethapi): implement eth_getBlockReceipts
Infestor.body:
### Description

Addition of a new ETH RPC method `eth_getBlockReceipts`.

https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/pull/438

Standardized and implemented in several eth clients e.g. geth, nethermind, nimbus, besu e.t.c.

### Other changes

None.

### Tested

[`internal/ethapi/api.go`](https://app.codecov.io/gh/celo-org/celo-blockchain/blob/master/internal%2Fethapi%2Fapi.go) is not covered by any tests.

To be tested on a devnet/testnet with existing chaindata.

### Related issues

 Resolves #2187

### Backwards compatibility

Backwards compatible.
_commit_messages:
feat (internal/ethapi): implement eth_getBlockReceipts

* ref: #2187
inline-docs (eth-client): add description for BlockReceipts

refactor (ethapi/api.go): generateReceiptResponse helper

* generateReceiptResponse now receives a context and a backend interface so as to also compute the effectiveGasPrice field in transaction receipts
Merge branch 'master' into sohail/feat-ethapi-getblockreceipts
reconcile: receipt changes from master 272982b (#2278)

Source.title: eth/downloader: save the correct delivery time for state sync
Source.body: The state sync delivery time was accounted as the time the response reached the trie node data processor. But that's wrong, because the processor might be blocked processing a previous response from someone else. That would mean that with enough load on the processor, the delivery times will get pushed more and more out, causing the RTT calculations to be totally off.
_commit_messages: eth/downloader: save the correct delivery time for state sync

Source.title: cmd/geth: fix bad genesis test
Source.body:
This PR fixes the genesis tests that were flaky. 
The tests spat out 
```
  test_cmd.go:250: (stderr) Fatal: Failed to write genesis block: genesis has no chain configuration
```
The first test should have failed ever since https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/commit/37dd9086ec491900311fc39837f4a62ef5fd3a4a, three years ago. 

However,  _after_  a test unsuccessfully did a `geth init` (thus, not writing the genesis successfully) the test continued, and started geth, -- which then used the _default_ init-config: the mainnet. 

And the tests passed, because the query checked the genesis nonce. And the testcases were using the same nonce as mainnet does. Doh!

Tests are now changed to use other nonces. I also removed the first test, since we also have a _different_ test to ensure that we reject genesis:es without configs: https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/blob/master/core/genesis_test.go#L68
_commit_messages: cmd/geth/test: fix genesis bad test
